title: GitHub Profile, Repository Health, and Verifiable Work · Hiring Practice Room
summary: A guided career studio extends selective GitHub evidence route while retaining truthful evidence, accessibility, privacy, security, consent, and learner agency. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of selective GitHub evidence route while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select a small set of projects by target-role relevance, independent contribution, evidence strength, recency, and safe disclosure.
Write a concise profile route that helps a reviewer reach role, proof, boundaries, contact choice, and accessible alternatives.
Create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Project Selection
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The workshop variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Project Selection.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select a small set of projects by target-role relevance, independent contribution, evidence strength, recency, and safe disclosure. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Project Selection, keep Project Selection connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The workshop variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Project Selection condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: CareerOneStop Salary Finder — Controls location- and occupation-specific compensation research while requiring total-package, source-date, sample, and local-law caveats.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Project Selection, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Project Selection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Commit to the mission model · Workshop Project Selection, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The workshop variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Project Selection, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: More pinned repositories always create a stronger profile.

options.text: For commit to the mission model, apply Project Selection without its stated preconditions, omit the selective GitHub evidence route trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Project Selection for selective GitHub evidence route.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Reproducible Proof
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Reproducible Proof: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The workshop variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Reproducible Proof.
steps.why: Retrieving Reproducible Proof through the concrete selective GitHub evidence route case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Reproducible Proof, keep Reproducible Proof connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Reproducible Proof, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Reproducible Proof precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Reproducible Proof, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The workshop variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Reproducible Proof, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A passing historical workflow badge proves the project works now.
